snippets.title: OpenClaw Skill Template
snippets.description: Standard OpenClaw skill structure with SKILL.md frontmatter
snippets.code:
---
name: {{skill_name}}
description: "{{description}}"
version: 1.0.0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    tags: [{{category}}]
    category: {{category}}
    auto_load: false
---

# {{skill_name}}

{{description}}

## Features

- Feature 1
- Feature 2

## Usage

```python
from {{skill_name}} import Skill
skill = Skill()
result = skill.execute()
```

snippets.title: SQLite Database Setup
snippets.description: Standard SQLite database initialization with tables
snippets.code:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path

def init_db(db_path: str):
    """Initialize SQLite database."""
    Path(db_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    
    with sqlite3.connect(db_path) as conn:
        conn.execute('''
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS items (
                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
                name TEXT NOT NULL,
                created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
            )
        ''')
        conn.commit()
    
    return db_path

snippets.title: Markdown Report Generator
snippets.description: Generate formatted Markdown reports with tables
snippets.code:
from datetime import datetime
from typing import List, Dict

def generate_markdown_report(
    title: str,
    data: List[Dict],
    columns: List[str]
) -> str:
    """Generate Markdown table report."""
    lines = [
        f'# {title}',
        '',
        f'Generated: {datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")}',
        '',
        '| ' + ' | '.join(columns) + ' |',
        '|' + '|'.join(['---' for _ in columns]) + '|'
    ]
    
    for row in data:
        values = [str(row.get(col, '')) for col in columns]
        lines.append('| ' + ' | '.join(values) + ' |')
    
    return '\n'.join(lines)

snippets.title: CLI Argument Parser
snippets.code:
import argparse
from typing import Optional

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog='my-tool',
        description='Tool description',
        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
        epilog='Example: my-tool --input file.txt'
    )
    
    parser.add_argument('input', help='Input file')
    parser.add_argument('-o', '--output', help='Output file')
    parser.add_argument('-v', '--verbose', action='store_true', help='Verbose output')
    
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    # Your logic here
    print(f'Processing: {args.input}')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

snippets.title: Cron Job Template
snippets.description: Python script template for scheduled tasks
snippets.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Scheduled task script.
Run with: python script.py
Or add to crontab: 0 */6 * * * /usr/bin/python3 /path/to/script.py
"""

import logging
from datetime import datetime

logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
    handlers=[
        logging.FileHandler('logs/task.log'),
        logging.StreamHandler()
    ]
)

def main():
    logging.info('Task started')
    
    try:
        # Your task logic here
        result = process_data()
        logging.info(f'Task completed: {result}')
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f'Task failed: {e}')
        raise

def process_data():
    """Main task logic."""
    pass

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

snippets.title: Dataclass Model
snippets.code:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List
from datetime import datetime

@dataclass\class Item:
    """Item data model."""
    name: str
    description: str = ''
    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.now)
    is_active: bool = True
    
    def validate(self) -> bool:
        """Validate item data."""
        if not self.name:
            raise ValueError('Name is required')
        if len(self.name) > 100:
            raise ValueError('Name too long')
        return True
    
    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        """Convert to dictionary."""
        return {
            'name': self.name,
            'description': self.description,
            'tags': self.tags,
            'created_at': self.created_at.isoformat(),
            'is_active': self.is_active
        }
